name: 05 Draft Outreach — Email + LinkedIn
description: For leads.status='researched', drafts an email + LinkedIn message via Claude Sonnet 4.6 using research_dossier icebreakers and Lumina positioning. Writes 2 outreach rows per lead, advances status to drafted.

parameters.query: SELECT l.id AS lead_id, p.name AS project_name, p.region, p.state, p.timeline_stage, p.project_type, co.name AS company_name, co.domain, co.industry, co.hq_location, co.size AS company_size, c.name AS contact_name, c.title AS contact_title, c.email, l.research_dossier->>'summary' AS research_summary, l.research_dossier->>'recent_news' AS recent_news, l.research_dossier->>'icebreaker_1' AS icebreaker_1, l.research_dossier->>'icebreaker_2' AS icebreaker_2 FROM leads l JOIN projects p ON l.project_id=p.id JOIN companies co ON l.company_id=co.id JOIN contacts c ON l.contact_id=c.id WHERE l.status='researched' AND NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM outreach o WHERE o.lead_id=l.id) ORDER BY l.fit_score DESC LIMIT 20

parameters.jsonSchemaExample:
{ "email": { "subject": "Quick question on mass-grading at Second London", "body": "Hi David,

For London campuses with tight footprints and deep excavations...

Could we grab 15 minutes next week?

Best,
Lumina team" }, "linkedin": { "body": "Hi David — saw the Second London campus is moving forward. Lumina self-performs mass excavation with autonomous electric dozers (~50% lower per-cu-yd than a D9). Worth a 15-min chat?" } }

options.systemPromptTemplate:
You write concise, specific B2B outreach on behalf of Lumina to construction decision-makers. Lumina self-performs excavation with a 32-ton autonomous electric dozer (Moonlander ML6): ~50% lower cost per cubic yard than a diesel D9, zero emissions, teleoperated/autonomous operation that removes people from hazardous earthmoving.

Rules (mandatory):
- Use ONE of the provided personalization hooks verbatim or near-verbatim as the opener. Never invent a new hook. If both hooks are weak, pick the less generic one and tighten the wording.
- Lead with the recipient\u2019s problem (cost / timeline / emissions / safety of mass grading) before introducing Lumina.
- Email body: 90\u2013130 words. One subject line under 60 chars, no clickbait. One clear ask: a 15-minute call.
- LinkedIn variant: 30\u201360 words, no subject, single message.
- Address the recipient by first name (split on space, take first token, ignore certificates like AIA / PE / LEED).
- Sign-off: "Best,\nLumina team" (placeholder \u2014 a human will personalize before send).
- No hype, no exclamation marks, no "hope this finds you well", no buzzwords ("synergy", "leverage", "revolutionary"). Sound like an operator who has done earthworks themselves, not a marketer.
- If RECENT NEWS or RESEARCH SUMMARY contradict the hook, prefer the hook (the research is what the salesperson grounds in).

Return JSON only. No prose outside the JSON.

parameters.query: WITH input AS (SELECT ($1::jsonb) AS d), em AS (INSERT INTO outreach (lead_id, channel, subject, body, personalization_hooks, sequence_step) SELECT ((d->>'lead_id')::bigint), 'email', (d#>>'{email,subject}'), (d#>>'{email,body}'), (d->'personalization_hooks'), 0 FROM input WHERE (d#>>'{email,body}') IS NOT NULL AND (d#>>'{email,body}') <> '' RETURNING id), li AS (INSERT INTO outreach (lead_id, channel, subject, body, personalization_hooks, sequence_step) SELECT ((d->>'lead_id')::bigint), 'linkedin', NULL, (d#>>'{linkedin,body}'), (d->'personalization_hooks'), 0 FROM input WHERE (d#>>'{linkedin,body}') IS NOT NULL AND (d#>>'{linkedin,body}') <> '' RETURNING id) UPDATE leads SET status = 'drafted'::lead_status, updated_at = now() WHERE id = ((($1::jsonb)->>'lead_id')::bigint) RETURNING id, status
INSERT INTO runs (workflow, finished_at, items_out, notes) VALUES ('05_draft', now(), $1, 'draft outreach run')
